reasoning:
The initial value on 2025-12-21 is 67.06, which is a recent local maximum compared to the H2 2025 average. Historically (e.g., in late 2024), the trailing 7-day average carries over strong mid-December data, leading to a slight increase by late December (in 2024, it rose from 67.40 on Dec 21 to 67.52 on Dec 28). The FRED statistical tool projected a ~69% chance of a short-term increase, but I've slightly discounted this because the latest daily prints showed a minor dip (down from 67.17 to 67.06 in the last 2 days), bringing my 1-week forecast to 63%. 

For the 1-month horizon (Jan 20, 2026), tech hiring typically experiences a minor new-year budget rollout effect, as evidenced by a jump in mid-January 2025. However, the current value of 67.06 represents a strong peak relative to recent months, making the hurdle somewhat challenging, so I shrink the probability closer to 56%. 

For the 3-month horizon (Mar 21, 2026), larger uncertainties dominate. In 2025, the index crashed from ~67 to ~62.5 by late March due to structural tech sector freezes rather than seasonality (since the index is seasonally adjusted). The overall macroeconomic consensus signals a soft landing but tech hiring is still considered "in the doldrums" (-33% vs Feb 2020). Without strong evidence of an explosive recovery, and with 67.06 being relatively high, the probability of exceeding this mark in three months converges closer to the 50/50 baseline, reflecting maximum uncertainty.
